title: STANDARD FOR FRESH GARLIC CXS 337-2020 Adopted in 2020.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR FRESH GARLIC CXS 337-2020 Adopted in 2020.
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Fresh Garlic CXS 337-2020, adopted in 2020.

Defines the standard's scope as quality requirements for garlic after preparation and packaging, allows slight loss of freshness/turgidity if applied after packaging, and makes the holder/seller responsible for not offering or marketing products that do not conform.

Defines the produce covered: commercial bulbs of Allium sativum (with or without skin), including multi‑clove and solo garlic, excluding green, undeveloped or industrial‑processing garlic, and specifies 'fresh', 'semi-dry' and 'dry' garlic by stem moisture and outer-skin dryness.

States the Standard applies to commercial Allium sativum bulbs with or without skin and allows some external skin dryness when present without detriment to clove freshness after preparation and packaging.

Specifies included products (multi-clove and solo garlic), excludes green/undeveloped/industrial garlic, and defines 'fresh', 'semi-dry' and 'dry' garlic according to stem and outer-skin moisture/dryness.

Sets minimum quality requirements (intact, sound, firm, clean, practically free of pests/visible roots/shoots/foreign matter/smells, free of abnormal moisture and temperature damage), requires appropriate root trimming and stem-length limits for dry/braided garlic, mandates adequate development to withstand transport, and classifies garlic into 'Extra', Class I and Class II with specified allowable minor defects for lower classes.

Lists the minimum requirements applicable to all classes: garlic must be intact (with outer skin where applicable), sound, firm, clean, practically pest-free, free of abnormal moisture/smells/temperature damage/visible shoots, practically free of roots, and able to withstand transport to destination in satisfactory condition.
Requires garlic to have reached an appropriate degree of development (maturity) according to criteria of the variety/commercial type and the area in which it was grown.
Introduces classification of garlic into three classes (Extra, Class I and Class II).

Defines the 'Extra' class: garlic must be of superior quality, characteristic of the variety/commercial type, essentially free of defects except very slight superficial ones that do not affect appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation, with bulbs of regular shape (properly cleaned) and compact cloves.

Defines Class I: garlic must be of good quality and characteristic of the variety/commercial type with reasonably compact cloves, allowing slight defects (e.g., slight tears in the outer skin or slight shape defects) provided they do not affect general appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation.

Defines Class II as garlic not qualifying for higher classes but meeting minimum requirements (Section 3.1) and lists allowable defects and limits including tears or missing outer skin up to half the surface, staining up to half the surface, no more than two damaged cloves, healed injuries, slight bruises, shape defects, and up to three missing cloves or one-fifth of total cloves (whichever is lower).

Specifies sizing: garlic may be sized by diameter or by existing trading practices (in which case the package must be labelled with size and method), and provides an optional table of size codes A–N with corresponding diameter ranges in mm (not applicable to solo garlic).

States that tolerances for quality and size are allowed per lot for produce not meeting the class indicated, failing produce may be resorted and brought into conformity per the Guidelines for Food Import Control System (CXG 47-2003), and summarizes the detailed class-specific quality and size tolerances that follow.

At all marketing stages, permits tolerances in quality and size for lots that do not meet the indicated class and allows failing produce to be resorted and brought into conformity in accordance with CXG 47-2003.
For the 'Extra' class, allows up to 5% by number or weight of bulbs that do not meet Extra but meet Class I, and within that tolerance no more than 1% may consist of produce meeting only Class II.

For Class I, allows up to 10% by number or weight of bulbs that do not meet Class I but meet Class II (within which up to 1% may be produce failing Class II or minimum requirements or affected by decay), and additionally limits externally visible sprouted cloves to no more than 1% by weight of bulbs.

For Class II, allows up to 10% by number or weight of bulbs that satisfy neither the class nor the minimum requirements (within which up to 2% may be affected by decay), and additionally limits bulbs with externally visible sprouts to no more than 5% by weight.
Size tolerances: for all classes, allows up to 10% by number or weight of bulbs not corresponding to the size indicated on the package.

Sets presentation requirements: packages must be uniform by origin/variety/quality/size with visible contents representative; packaging materials must protect produce, be clean and food-grade with non-toxic inks/glues for trade markings, packaging must comply with CXC 44-1995, and containers must meet quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance requirements and be free of foreign matter and odour.
Requires package contents to be uniform and contain only garlic of the same origin, variety or commercial type, quality and size, and that the visible part of bulk lots represent the entire contents.

Requires packaging that properly protects garlic using clean, food-grade materials that avoid damage and permits trade-marked paper or stamps only if printed with non-toxic ink or glue, and mandates compliance with the Code of Practice CXC 44-1995 for packaging and transport.

Specifies that containers must meet quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure proper handling, shipping and preservation of garlic and that packages or lots be free of foreign matter and smell.
Heading only: introduces provisions concerning marking and labelling but contains no substantive requirements in this section text.

Introductory heading noting that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), specific provisions apply to consumer packages (details follow in subsections).

Requires that if the produce is not visible from the outside, the consumer package be labelled with the name of the produce (e.g., 'garlic', 'fresh garlic', 'semi-dry garlic', 'dry garlic') and the variety.
Requires declaration of the country of origin and optionally the district or national/regional/local place name where the garlic was grown.

Requires non-retail packages to bear specified particulars legibly and indelibly on the same side or in accompanying documents, and for bulk transport those particulars must accompany the goods or be attached inside the transport vehicle, with an electronic solution allowed if machine-readable and easily accessible.
Requires the name and address of the exporter, packer and/or dispatcher to appear on the label and allows an optional identification code.
Requires the name of the produce when contents are not visible (e.g., 'garlic', 'fresh garlic', 'dry garlic', 'solo garlic') and permits optional declaration of variety or commercial type.
Requires declaration of the country of origin and optionally the district or national/regional/local place name where the produce was grown.

Requires commercial identification including class, size (expressed as minimum and maximum bulb diameters or a size code), and optional net weight; notes that a full or commonly used name should be indicated and that national legislation may require explicit name/address or use of a code mark with a reference to the packer/dispatcher.
